task_description: Screen each abstract for relevance to: machine learning for predicting drug-target binding affinity or drug-target interaction. Label RELEVANT if the paper applies ML or deep learning to predict whether a small molecule will bind to a protein target, or to estimate the binding affinity score. Label NOT_RELEVANT if the prediction task differs (e.g., toxicity, ADMET, drug synergy, disease classification) or if no ML is involved.

papers.title: Equivariant Graph Neural Network for Structure-Based Binding Affinity Prediction
papers.abstract: We develop an SE(3)-equivariant graph neural network that encodes protein-ligand co-crystal structures and predicts binding free energy. Trained on PDBbind v2020, the model achieves Pearson r=0.84 and RMSE 1.21 kcal/mol on the CASF-2016 benchmark, outperforming SchNet, DimeNet++, and conventional docking scores. An ablation study confirms that equivariant message-passing outperforms invariant alternatives by 0.07 in Pearson correlation.
papers.topic_hint: equivariant GNN, protein-ligand binding, PDBbind, CASF-2016, structure-based
papers.methodology_hint: SE(3)-equivariant GNN, PDBbind v2020, Pearson 0.84, CASF-2016, ablation
papers.claimed_contribution: SE(3)-equivariant GNN for structure-based binding affinity, Pearson 0.84 on CASF-2016.

papers.title: Sparse Bayesian Regression for Genome-Wide Variant Association with Type 2 Diabetes
papers.abstract: We apply spike-and-slab variational inference to GWAS data from 280,000 UK Biobank participants to identify causal variants for type 2 diabetes. The method identifies 51 novel loci while controlling false discovery rate. Cross-population replication is confirmed in an East Asian cohort. No drug or protein target is modelled; this is a statistical genetics study.
papers.topic_hint: Bayesian regression, GWAS, type 2 diabetes, UK Biobank, variant association
papers.methodology_hint: spike-and-slab VI, GWAS, 280k participants, T2D loci — statistical genetics not drug-target
papers.claimed_contribution: Spike-and-slab GWAS identifying 51 novel T2D loci with FDR control and cross-population replication.

papers.title: Transformer-Based Virtual Screening for Kinase Inhibitor Discovery
papers.abstract: We pre-train a molecular transformer on 130 million SMILES strings and fine-tune for binary classification of kinase binding activity across 372 kinase targets. The model achieves mean AUC 0.91 across targets on a held-out test set, outperforming fingerprint-based SVM and graph neural network baselines. An analysis of attention heads reveals that the model learns substrate-recognition motifs consistent with kinase biology.
papers.topic_hint: transformer, virtual screening, kinase inhibitor, drug-target binding, SMILES
papers.methodology_hint: molecular transformer, 372 kinase targets, AUC 0.91, SVM/GNN baseline, attention analysis
papers.claimed_contribution: Molecular transformer for kinase binding classification, mean AUC 0.91 across 372 targets.

papers.title: Neural Networks for Predicting Cytochrome P450 Inhibition and ADMET Properties
papers.abstract: Drug metabolism and safety profiling requires accurate prediction of cytochrome P450 inhibitory activity and ADMET properties. We train a multi-task neural network on 48 assay endpoints from ChEMBL and proprietary screening data. AUC 0.87 is achieved averaged across CYP450 isoforms. The prediction task is metabolic liability and safety, not drug-target binding affinity or interaction prediction.
papers.topic_hint: neural network, CYP450, ADMET, metabolism, drug safety, multi-task
papers.methodology_hint: multi-task NN, ChEMBL, AUC 0.87, CYP450 — ADMET/metabolism not binding affinity
papers.claimed_contribution: Multi-task NN for CYP450 and ADMET prediction, AUC 0.87, not binding affinity.

papers.title: Graph Attention Network for Drug-Target Interaction Prediction from Molecular and Protein Graphs
papers.abstract: We build dual graph attention networks encoding both molecular topology of the drug and structural features of the protein target residue graph. Interaction predictions are obtained by a bilinear attention readout. Trained on DrugBank and KEGG, the model achieves AUROC 0.92 and AP 0.89 on a held-out balanced test set, substantially outperforming fingerprint-similarity, matrix factorisation, and previous GNN baselines. Generalisation to unseen protein families is validated.
papers.topic_hint: graph attention network, drug-target interaction, molecular graph, protein graph, DrugBank
papers.methodology_hint: dual GAT, DrugBank+KEGG, AUROC 0.92, AP 0.89, unseen protein family generalisation
papers.claimed_contribution: Dual GAT for drug-target interaction prediction, AUROC 0.92, generalising to unseen protein families.

papers.title: Deep Learning for Automated Grading of Knee Osteoarthritis from X-Rays
papers.abstract: We train a convolutional neural network on 23,000 knee radiographs from the OAI dataset to predict Kellgren-Lawrence osteoarthritis grades. The model achieves weighted kappa 0.76, matching radiologist inter-reader agreement. Grad-CAM visualisations highlight joint space narrowing and osteophyte regions. This work involves medical imaging and musculoskeletal disease, not drug-target binding prediction.
papers.topic_hint: CNN, knee osteoarthritis, X-ray, Kellgren-Lawrence grading, OAI
papers.methodology_hint: CNN, OAI 23k radiographs, kappa 0.76, Grad-CAM — orthopaedic imaging not drug-target
papers.claimed_contribution: CNN for KL osteoarthritis grading from X-rays, kappa 0.76, with radiologist agreement.

papers.title: Binding Affinity Estimation via Pretrained Protein Language Models and Molecular Fingerprints
papers.abstract: Protein language models trained on evolutionary sequence data encode rich structural information useful for binding prediction. We combine ESM-2 protein embeddings with Morgan fingerprints and train a gradient boosting regressor to predict pIC50 binding affinity across 380 assays in the ChEMBL bioassay database. Mean Pearson r=0.77 is achieved. The approach generalises better to novel protein families than structure-based methods requiring co-crystal structures.
papers.topic_hint: protein language model, ESM-2, binding affinity, pIC50, gradient boosting, ChEMBL
papers.methodology_hint: ESM-2 embeddings, Morgan fingerprints, gradient boosting, pIC50, Pearson 0.77, ChEMBL
papers.claimed_contribution: ESM-2 + Morgan fingerprint regressor for pIC50 binding affinity, Pearson 0.77, sequence-only.

papers.title: Variational Inference for Patient Clustering from Longitudinal ICU Data
papers.abstract: ICU patient trajectories are heterogeneous and clustering reveals clinically meaningful phenotypes. We apply a variational deep embedding model to 72-hour vital sign and lab sequences from 32,000 MIMIC-IV stays. Six stable ICU phenotypes are identified, each with distinct organ failure patterns and mortality rates. Clinical validation confirms the phenotypes are actionable. No drug-target data or binding prediction is involved.
papers.topic_hint: variational inference, patient clustering, ICU, longitudinal, MIMIC-IV
papers.methodology_hint: variational deep embedding, MIMIC-IV, ICU phenotyping — patient clustering not drug-target
papers.claimed_contribution: Variational embedding for ICU phenotyping, 6 clusters with distinct mortality profiles, no drug-target.

papers.title: Contrastive Pre-training for Improved Drug-Target Affinity Prediction with Limited Labels
papers.abstract: Large binding affinity datasets are available for some target classes but sparse for others. We apply contrastive pre-training on 4.2 million unlabelled drug-target pairs from ChEMBL and BindingDB to learn representations before fine-tuning on task-specific affinity labels. In low-label regimes (100 labelled pairs), the pre-trained model outperforms fully supervised baselines by 15% in Pearson r. Evaluation follows the Davis and KIBA benchmark protocols.
papers.topic_hint: contrastive pre-training, drug-target affinity, label-efficient, Davis, KIBA, ChEMBL
papers.methodology_hint: contrastive pre-training, 4.2M pairs, Davis/KIBA benchmarks, Pearson +15% low-label regime
papers.claimed_contribution: Contrastive pre-training for drug-target affinity, +15% Pearson in low-label settings, Davis/KIBA benchmarks.

papers.title: A Survey of Machine Learning Methods for Antibiotic Resistance Surveillance
papers.abstract: Antimicrobial resistance surveillance relies on phenotypic susceptibility testing augmented by genomic prediction. This survey covers 140 papers applying ML to predict minimum inhibitory concentrations from whole-genome sequencing, plasmid detection, and resistome profiling. We categorise methods, summarise benchmark performance, and identify gaps. No new ML model is developed and no drug-target binding or affinity data is used.
papers.topic_hint: survey, antibiotic resistance, ML, MIC prediction, genomic surveillance
papers.methodology_hint: survey 140 papers, antibiotic resistance surveillance — no new model, no drug-target binding
papers.claimed_contribution: Survey of ML for antibiotic resistance surveillance across 140 papers, no new model.
